title: Filesystem Dispatch: Repair a Broken Project Handoff
summary: Triage a project with nested duplicate folders, hidden extensions, case mismatches, absolute paths, and misleading browser symptoms.
objectives: Repair a portable directory tree without losing the original evidence.
Discriminate filesystem defects from network and rendering defects.

steps.title: Diagnose the moved image · zipped-resource-site dispatch
steps.instruction: In the zipped-resource-site dispatch, why does the current source reference fail on another learner’s computer?
steps.why: Portable paths preserve the relationship between project resources when the containing folder moves. Working through diagnose the moved image narrows the zipped-resource-site dispatch while you inspect the artifact.

options.text: SVG files can only load from a desktop folder.
It names one user’s absolute machine location instead of the project-relative image location.

steps.title: Correct the mental model · zipped-resource-site dispatch

options.text: HTML is executable machine code that runs without an application.
HTML is document data interpreted by a browser process running with operating-system and hardware support.
HTML is a physical display protocol sent directly to the monitor.

steps.title: Separate source and DOM · zipped-resource-site dispatch
steps.instruction: In the zipped-resource-site dispatch, which statement accounts for the recovered tree?
steps.why: Source, the browser-built DOM, layout, and pixels are related stages rather than interchangeable evidence. Working through separate source and dom narrows the zipped-resource-site dispatch as you connect the explanation to evidence.

content.text: A browser parses source into object models, applies error-recovery rules, derives accessibility information and styles, computes layout, and paints output. Source shows the input text; the DOM shows the current parsed structure; computed styles and layout show resolved presentation; pixels show only the final visual result. Apply this distinction during the zipped-resource-site dispatch read task.

hints: During zipped-resource-site dispatch read, identify which browser pipeline boundary “Separate source and DOM” asks about.
Compare every zipped-resource-site dispatch option with the named “Separate source and DOM” evidence during read.
For zipped-resource-site dispatch read, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Separate source and DOM.”

options.text: Source and DOM must remain identical character for character after parsing.
Source is parser input; the DOM is the browser’s current constructed representation and may include recovery.
The DOM is a screenshot made from the final pixels.

steps.title: Rebuild load journey · zipped-resource-site dispatch
steps.instruction: Within the zipped-resource-site dispatch, reconstruct the load journey in causal order.
steps.why: Local-file access and web requests can display similar pages but fail at different boundaries. Ordering the zipped-resource-site dispatch exposes its first unsupported causal handoff.

content.text: Do not order by vocabulary or visual position. Test whether each event creates the conditions required by the next event in the zipped-resource-site dispatch.

hints: In the zipped-resource-site dispatch sequence, locate the first event whose required input is not ready.
Separate zipped-resource-site dispatch load journey events from later visible outcomes.
Rebuild the zipped-resource-site dispatch sequence one causal handoff at a time.

options.text: The learner enters an HTTPS URL
The browser sends a request toward the identified server
The server returns a status, headers, and resource bytes
The browser processes the response and renders the document

steps.title: Write the evidence note · zipped-resource-site dispatch
steps.instruction: Write a concise case note for the zipped-resource-site dispatch. Explain the mechanism, cite observed evidence, and reject one plausible alternative.
steps.why: Explaining the zipped-resource-site dispatch from memory exposes gaps that selected answers can hide. Cite the mechanism and its evidence.

content.title: Transfer brief
content.text: A teammate must be able to reproduce your conclusion without seeing the earlier answer choices. Name evidence and limits, not only the final verdict.

hints: Start the zipped-resource-site dispatch case note with the earliest observed event.
Use these zipped-resource-site dispatch technical anchors where they fit: folder, relative path, extension, operating system, browser.
Close the zipped-resource-site dispatch note by naming one alternative and the evidence that rules it out.
